name: Joe Zawinul
abstract: Josef Erich Zawinul ( ZOV-in-əl; 7 July 1932 – 11 September 2007) was an Austrian jazz and jazz fusion keyboardist and composer. First coming to prominence with saxophonist Cannonball Adderley, Zawinul went on to play with Miles Davis and to become one of the creators of jazz fusion, a musical genre that combined jazz with rock.

about_quotes.speaker: Wilson "Willie T" Turbinton
about_quotes.evidence: Me and Joe Zawinul became real good friends through the years.

about_quotes.speaker: Louis Hayes
about_quotes.evidence: And then really the major pianist was Joe Zawinul. He was there for years, at least ten years.

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Jones
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Joe Zawinul in the context of Weather Report and other musicians, but the interviewee (Sam Jones) does not respond to these specific mentions with personal anecdotes or confirm a direct professional relationship in the provided text.

about_quotes.speaker: Wayne Shorter
about_quotes.evidence: [Appelbaum] For some of the musicians you’ve been talking about today, whether it’s Art Blakey or Miles Davis or maybe Joe Zawinul or Charlie Parker...do you think they all had Buddha nature in them? [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah. Oh, yeah.
